text: Hello, this is a new video for a new case of the difficult condition that we treated during the week of the stroke. The patient had previously suffered from a lot of pain, four previous post-operative operations, and a concussion due to the incidence of weaknesses in the previous operation. She was also told by all operations that she was suffering from heart attacks. Unfortunately, he suffers from chronic pain in the pelvis due to the existence of a complex case of six centimeters on the left side.
The surgeon enters the abdominal cavity at the wang point, located midway between the posterior abdomen and the anterior chest cavity, to begin the surgical procedure.

As you saw in the beginning of the video, there was a curtain of shells surrounding the intestines that blocked the vision from the uterus area. After dealing with it, we found these precise intestines covered inside the stomach wall. Since the patient does not suffer from previous symptoms, due to the presence of these types of splinters, so we decided to cooperate and consult with the surgeon who was present, the surgeon of the digestive system who was present for consultation, that there is no need for these fine marks.

The surgeon uses scissors to dissect and separate splinters from the abdominal wall, focusing on the connection of a mass with the side wall of the pelvis. The yellow tissue is being lowered from the mass as the dissection progresses.
The surgeon continues to dismantle adhesions along the so-called straight line, gradually separating the connections to reveal the boundaries of the masses.

The surgeon navigates strong adhesions in the pelvic region, using a protective screen to avoid damaging the intestines while attempting to separate the rectum from surrounding tissue. The boundaries of the affected area are now clearly visible.
The surgeon begins to collect and secure the visible bag-like structure, carefully avoiding deeper penetration into the surrounding area to minimize potential damage.

The surgeon completes the extraction of the mass, ensuring no residual liquid remains by pressing with a screen. A plastic bag is placed to contain the screen and the extracted mass, preventing contact with the uterus wall during the procedure.
The surgeon inserts a Surgicil mesh into the surgical site to prevent chemical interactions that could lead to contamination. A drain tube is placed to release a blood clot from the area.
The operation ended safely and the patient went to the hospital on the second day and was in good condition. Thank you for watching.
